question.text: The final versions of SID and SAI, after incorporating SEBI's observations, are made available on:

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** After SEBI reviews the draft SID/SAI and provides observations, the AMC incorporates those observations and the final versions are made available on the AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India) website for public access.
**The Trap:** The SEBI website hosts the DRAFT versions, not the final versions. This draft-on-SEBI, final-on-AMFI distinction is a classic exam trap. RBI and NSE have no role in hosting mutual fund scheme documents.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NISM V-B and XII test this document hosting distinction. Remember: SEBI = drafts for review, AMFI = finals for investor access.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Bookmark the AMFI website (amfiindia.com) — it is your single most important resource for accessing the latest SID, SAI, and KIM of any scheme from any AMC. It also provides NAV data, performance data, and investor education resources.
